title: MYC cancer wall breaks, AI screeners pick their own drafts, humans misidentify AI art - TCR 05/17/26
description:
An OHSU team disabled the MYC cancer protein's hidden DNA-repair function with a small-molecule compound after 40 years of "undruggable" assumptions, a peer-reviewed Maryland-Singapore-Ohio State study measured AI resume screeners picking their own drafts 23 to 60 percent more often, and 4,000 Fisker Ocean owners reverse-engineered a bankrupt manufacturer's software stack to keep 11,000 stranded EVs running.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-17-2026/

0:00 MYC cancer protein's hidden DNA-repair job disabled
1:29 When Forty Years Looked Like One Problem
4:24 AI resume screeners self-prefer 23 to 60 percent
7:40 Fisker owners rebuild a bankrupt carmaker
11:21 Real Monet misread as AI-generated
14:30 A real Monet confidently misread as AI

This episode covers the OHSU MYC DNA-repair separability paper in Genes & Development, the Xu et al. AI evaluator self-preference measurement across GPT-4, Claude, and Gemini, the Fisker Owners Association reverse-engineering of the Ocean SUV's CAN bus, the Gofar Pacific transform fault dilatancy-strengthening discovery, and a real Monet Water Lilies being confidently misread as AI-generated.

For forty years MYC has been treated as one of oncology's most defeated targets - overexpressed in half of all human cancers but considered pharmacologically unreachable. The OHSU paper shows MYC has been doing a second, separable job: physically participating in homologous-recombination DNA repair inside cancer cells, letting tumors silently survive chemotherapy and radiation. A small-molecule compound disabled the repair function without disturbing MYC's normal activity in healthy tissue. The wall the field had described as structural was a resolution problem. The same logic that opened KRAS earlier this month is now opening MYC. The Xu et al. evaluator study put a peer-reviewed number on closed-loop bias in AI hiring - the same model picks its own drafts 23 to 60 percent more often than equivalents, the effect held across three commercial frontier systems, and the corrective architecture is already being prototyped. The Fisker case proved the assumption that owners cannot replace a bankrupt manufacturer is empirically wrong, with a Discord server's worth of unpaid engineers running what used to require a car company. The Gofar fault work identified dilatancy strengthening as the physical mechanism stopping ruptures, making seismic hazard barriers mappable from geophysical surveys worldwide. And a genuine century-old Monet, posted on X as AI-generated, drew hundreds of viewers confidently citing its brushwork and composition as proof of machine origin - the same perceptual merger documented in April when 97% of listeners could not distinguish AI-generated music, now reaching back into a canvas painted before anyone alive was born.
